title: agathis robusta subsp. palmerstonii
intro: Agathis robusta subsp. palmerstonii, also known as the Kauri tree or Agathis, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the island of New Caledonia. The medicinal properties of Agathis robusta subsp. palmerstonii are attributed to its bark, which contains a variety of bioactive compounds including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Agathis robusta subsp. palmerstonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Agathis robusta subsp. palmerstonii is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
